ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,

translated: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And then, being warned in a dream, he went to the region of Galilee.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: “Honsakra ho adwene, na soro ahennie he abɛne.”
translated: "Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see the kingdom of heaven."

ref: After Jesus had gone without eating for
text: Odii mmuada adaduannan, awia ne anadwo, na yei akyi he kɔm dee no.
translated: God created fourteen days, day and night, and after that hunger came.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.

translated: Neither shall you swear by the earth, for it is his footstool; neither by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.

translated: Therefore, it is as if they are telling the ancestors that, 'Do not boast, but give your glory to the Lord.'

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.

translated: That which I want to explain about it is that, You should love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Honhwɛ yie na hoannyɔ ho tenenee nnipa anim sɛ bebehu ho; sɛ hoyɔ saa a hoonnya akatua ho Agya a ɔya soro he hɔ.
translated: Be careful not to do your righteous deeds before people to be seen by them; if you do so, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.

translated: When you give alms, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating

translated: Therefore, if you do not forgive others their trespasses, your Father who is in heaven will also not forgive you your trespasses. Explanation of the Parables

translated: Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.

translated: Truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it. Blessed are those who understand

translated: But you say that if someone tells his father or mother, 'What you would have received from me is a gift dedicated to God,' then that person does not need to honor his father and mother.

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.

translated: But he did not answer her at all. And his disciples came and begged him saying, "Send her away, for she is crying out after us too much."

text: na ɔyɛɛ nnipadɔm he nwanwa berɛ a behuu nea na ɔyɛ mmum he sɛ ɔɔkasa, bɛ a na bɛyɛ mpakye ne mmubuafoɔ sɛ bɛnnante, na nea na ɔyɛ anifirani he huu sɛ afie deɛ ohu adeɛ. Na bɛhyɛɛ Israel Nyankopɔn he animuonyam. Yesu maa nnipa mpemnan aduane (Marko

translated: And Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of him, and the boy was healed from that very hour.

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.

translated: And when the man saw that he could not prevail against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh; and the hollow of Jacob's thigh was out of joint, as he wrestled with him.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.

translated: And the master was angry and handed him over to the jailers until he should pay back all that he owed.

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?

translated: Someone says, "Listen carefully, in the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth"

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Kuruwaa a mɛnom he deɛ ho nso hobɛnom, nso sɛ hobɛtena me nsa nifa ne benkum so deɛ, ɔnnyɛ me ne mede bɛma, na mmom, ɔya hɔ ma bɛ a m’ Agya asiesie de ama bɛ.”
translated: Then he said to them, "You will indeed drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left is not for me to grant. These places belong to those for whom they have been prepared by my Father."

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.

translated: The father went to the second one and said the same thing to him; and his son answered, 'Sir, I will go'; but he did not go.

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”

translated: They said to him, "He will destroy those wicked people miserably, and will give the vineyard to other tenants who will give him the fruits in their season."

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.

translated: "Teacher, Moses said, 'If a man marries and has no child and dies, his brother should marry the widow and raise children for his dead brother.'"

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”

translated: Therefore go to the street corners, and invite to the wedding banquet anyone you find.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”

translated: And the second which is like it is: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:

translated: The scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat;

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?

translated: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold, or the temple that makes the gold sacred?

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?

translated: Which of these is greater! The gold itself or the temple, the gift itself or the altar that sanctifies the gift?

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.

translated: For I have chosen this time to use it in tribulation, and to endure it, and for my religion's sake all suffering will pass away.

translated: Let the one who goes to the farm return home to take clothes.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.

translated: But concerning that day and hour no one knows, neither the angels of heaven, nor the Son, except the Father alone.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.

translated: Truly I tell you, he will put that servant in charge of all his possessions.
